entry.title: Washington Post: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • 5 countries hit hard by the grain crisis in Ukraine
  • Israel and Egypt sign gas export deal as Europe seeks Russia alternative
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Ukraine takes painful losses in east to wear down Russia, Zelensky says
  • For Colombia’s candidates, WhatsApp mockery can be good publicity
  • As K-pop behemoth BTS hits pause, its fandom reels
  • Dom Phillips: Telling the story of Brazil, the Amazon and its peoples
  • Swiss airspace reopens after computer failure grounds flights for hours
  • ‘Zero covid’ has many in China dreaming of leaving
  • The case for and against Biden visiting Saudi Arabia
  • Russia made nearly $100B from fuel exports in war’s first 100 days, report says
  • Casualties rise as Russia makes incremental gains in east, Pentagon official says
  • Ukraine battle intensifies as Western backers mull new military aid
  • Hundreds of little penguins are washing up dead on New Zealand shores
  • U.K. cancels flight to deport asylum seekers to Rwanda after court challenges
  • Ukraine war brings peace — between Canada and Denmark
  • Brazil apologizes for telling journalist’s family bodies were found
  • Navalny reportedly moved to high-security prison infamous for abuse
  • Global nuclear arsenal expected to grow for first time since Cold War
  • Why Britain’s plan to scrap part of the Brexit deal is so controversial
  • Syria says Damascus airport operations suspended after Israeli strikes
  • 7 earthquakes strike off Iranian coast; no damage reported
  • Iran says rocket launch coming after photos show preparation
  • Israel hopes Biden trip helps tighten ties with Saudi Arabia
  • Iran arrests suspect allegedly involved in Tehran hacking
  • 6 dead in China as rain triggers landslides, house collapse
  • Torn from his first love in the Korean War, strangers just helped them reunite
  • Video contradicts police in Texas boy’s shooting death, family says
  • The president’s men and women, under interrogation
  • The gun deal could close the ‘boyfriend loophole.’ Here’s what it is.
  • N.C. school leaders accused of ignoring abuse have immunity, court rules
  • Post Politics Now: Trump notches several primary victories; Biden to celebrate Pride Month
  • Primary elections live updates: Tom Rice ousted in S.C. after Trump impeachment vote, and the GOP scores a win in Texas
  • Publix heiress paid Kimberly Guilfoyle’s $60,000 speaking fee on Jan. 6
  • Jan. 6 panel divided over whether to issue criminal referral of Trump
  • Seven ways you can financially prepare for a recession
  • We’re still not in a recession despite the stock market drop
  • SBA approved loans with signs of fraud early in pandemic, House report says
  • Coinbase’s layoffs suggest crypto’s problems are spreading
  • Warehouses in China and U.S. show global economy struggling to adjust
  • Future of Work: Rite Aid wants to put your pharmacist in your pocket
  • Water sensors can help you maintain a dry basement
  • The Boro in Tysons gets expansion approval for residential development
  • How to add color to concrete
  • Finding inspiration and relaxation in luxury condos of Old Town North
  • Macron Tech Champion Atos Faces Brutal New M&A World
  • Senators Treat Crypto Like a Digital New Jersey Turnpike
  • The Fed Has a Narrow Path Between Inflation and Recession
  • Federal Reserve Must Do More Than Raise Rates by 75 Points
  • Why Japan’s Yen Is the Weakest in 20 Years and What That Means
  • The Boom in Private Markets Has Transformed Finance. Here’s How
  • Why Closer Ties Between Russia and China Have Democracies Worried
  • The U.S. must oppose India’s rising Islamophobia
  • Your questions about covid-19, answered by Dr. Leana Wen
  • Democrats can’t have it both ways about the threat of GOP extremism
  • Can America ‘do big things’ again? Ask the regulators and lawyers.
  • A wave of violence as the court prepares to rule
  • Is AI sentient? Wrong question.
  • No Republican candidate should be able to escape the Jan. 6 hearings
  • Has Justin Trudeau been ‘Americanized’ — or is he just left-wing?
  • Fairfax County is a leader in promoting red-flag laws. Others should follow.
  • Is Virginia’s lieutenant governor paying attention to her friends?
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘Let’s call a spade a spade – this is illegal’: EU resumes legal action against UK over Northern Ireland protocol – live
  • Royal Ascot 2022: Prince of Wales’s Stakes and more on day two – live!
  • Sturgeon plans to hold second Scottish independence referendum in 2023
  • World Cup 2022 power rankings: breaking down the final 32

  • In failing to take a stand on Rwanda or rail strikes, Labour leaves itself open to attack | Owen Jones
  • Parts of John Hughes’ novel The Dogs copied from The Great Gatsby and Anna Karenina

  • Pleasure review – porn industry drama explores complex questions of consent

  • Owner of UK chip designer Arm may float some of firm’s shares in London
  • To infinity – actually, let’s go back: why can film franchises no longer look to the future?
  • Tyson Fury says he will ‘100%’ return to the boxing ring if the money is right
  • New data reveals extraordinary global heating in the Arctic
  • Stanley Cup final 2022: five things to know before Lightning v Avalanche
  • ‘I swear I saw faces in the darkness’: can you scare yourself happy?
  • A knight’s tale: Brindley Sherratt on the stamina and storytelling of Wagner’s Parsifal

  • NHS risks losing black and Asian doctors over ‘intolerable’ racism levels – report

  • Grenfell Tower fire: fifth anniversary marked with silent march – in pictures

  • ‘Starve and shiver with Sunak’ is the reality for millions. The chancellor can – and must – stop it | Gordon Brown

  • Vroom with a view: bikers, Basel and bare bottoms – in pictures

  • More than 20 million farm animals die on way to abattoir in US every year
  • ‘The Beatles? I was more a fan of the Beach Boys’: Peter Blake at 90 on pop art and clubbing with the Fab Four
  • Britain’s attack on its own protocol is one more exercise in Brexit gaslighting | Fintan O'Toole

  • Haze Fan: China says Bloomberg staffer released on bail earlier this year

  • Living with long Covid – podcast
  • Dom Phillips and Bruno Pereira: police in Brazil arrest second man for ‘alleged murder’
  • A brother’s 36-year fight against one of New Zealand’s worst miscarriages of justice
  • Sea level rise in England ‘will put 200,000 homes at risk by 2050’
  • Trump loses late bid to avoid testifying in New York fraud investigation
  • England fans finally have a stick to beat Gareth Southgate with | Barney Ronay
  • US defence contractor in talks to take over NSO Group’s hacking technology
  • Big-hitting Bairstow helps England to get risky and cast off old shackles | Andy Bull

  • What are the best Italian cookbooks? | Kitchen aide
  • Serena Williams granted wildcard into Wimbledon singles draw
  • Compulsory naps and an Ascot hat: Tuesday’s best photos
  • Pope Francis says Ukraine war was ‘perhaps somehow provoked’
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Global equity funds decline over 20% this year
  • New private home sales rise 105.5% in May, hit six-month high
  • Thai central bank ready to manage excessive baht volatility
  • Binance opens 2,000 positions for hiring - CEO
  • Construction manager jailed over worker's death during demolition works
  • Japan PM Kishida expects BOJ to stick to 2% inflation goal
  • EV battery startup Alsym aims to slash cost, eliminate lithium and cobalt
  • UK confident it can overturn legal ban on Rwanda plan
  • Heavy traffic through Woodlands and Tuas Checkpoints expected for the rest of June: ICA
  • Yen slump may open scope for Japan's central bank to tweak policy
  • As yen tumbles, Japan's automakers take cost burden off their suppliers
  • Manulife Asia on track for 2025 earning goal despite economic weakness
  • Veteran prosecutor G Kannan, 52, dies in an accident overseas
  • China car sales up 54% in the week of June 6 vs month earlier -CPCA
  • More than 100 CEOs urge US Congress to pass China competition bill
  • Malaysia lifts export ban on certain chicken products - ministry
  • Qualcomm wins fight against US$1 billion EU antitrust fine
  • Four men arrested after police officer assaulted at England game
  • Creditors to vote on Garuda Indonesia debt restructuring on Friday
  • Forever 21 takes third crack at China with new bricks and mortar store
  • Indonesia’s Jokowi reshuffles Cabinet for the third time in his current term
  • Tickets sold out for some peak-hour JB-Woodlands trains ahead of service resumption
  • Australia wants 'broad, deep' security ties with Japan, minister says
  • China's middle class looks to flee as COVID-19 policies bite
  • South Korea truckers return to work after strike ends; shares in companies rise
  • US rebuffs China by calling Taiwan Strait an international waterway
  • Commentary: Under shadow of Ukraine war, Shangri-La Dialogue shifts from peace to power

  • China attack on Taiwan would hit global trade more than Ukraine war, says Taiwan
  • South Korea truckers end strike; government agrees to extend pay guarantee
  • Children killed, tortured in Myanmar junta's crackdown, UN expert says
  • Shanghai Disney Resort to reopen Disneytown, hotel on Jun 16

  • Bitcoin tumbles to new 18-month low
  • China Southern Boeing 737 MAX makes test flights as domestic demand picks up
  • European smartphone market falls 12% in Q1, Counterpoint says
  • Japan govt bond futures collapse, yields test band despite BOJ heavy hand
  • Drivers shrug off soaring fuel prices, for now
  • Kampung chicken back at wet markets at higher prices, stallholders say some customers still willing to pay
  • Sentosa, RWS to install solar panels at 18 locations as part of carbon neutrality efforts
  • CNA remains most trusted news brand in Singapore: Reuters Institute report
  • HungryGoWhere resumes operations after Grab buys, relaunches F&B site
  • Vivian Balakrishnan in New Delhi for special meeting of India and ASEAN foreign ministers
  • Dengue outbreak: Wolbachia mosquito project to be expanded to 1,400 more HDB blocks following 'promising' results
  • She's hooked: The 19-year-old Singaporean crochet artist designing clothes and even S$333 bags
  • She didn't know she was autistic until she was 42. It changed her life
  • Sports Hub 'an isolated case'; other public-private partnerships in Singapore have worked well: Analysts
  • Singapore wrap up Asian Cup qualifying campaign with 6-2 thumping of Myanmar
  • Singapore may have access to new dengue vaccine in about a year, says veteran infectious diseases expert
  • Rare 'strawberry supermoon' seen over Singapore
  • Serial voyeur jailed for peeping at teen in Tanglin Shopping Centre toilet
  • Bento pleased that South Korea stuck to their style in friendlies
  • New Zealand need a better diet of competitive football-coach
  • Cummins plays down hip niggle, says Starc won't be rushed back
  • England fined for slow over rate in second test win over New Zealand
  • Costa Rica coach glad he kept faith with veterans

  • Rossi relishes Hungary's 'miracle' win over England
  • Ireland to bring five uncapped players on New Zealand tour
  • New Zealand's Jamieson ruled out of third England test with back injury
  • Triple Olympic champion Wlodarczyk to miss rest of season
  • New Zealand coach Hay lashes out at ref, VAR after World Cup exit
  • Ferrari find 'short-term fix' for hydraulics problem
  • Five-star Germany crush Italy to snap winless run
  • Batshuayi on target as Belgium beat Poland
  • Collins wonder goal in vain as Ukraine hold Ireland

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 15 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-15 10:53AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Migrant workers’ groups slam Hong Kong government for excluding domestic helpers in second batch of consumption vouchers
  • Hong Kong’s Greater Bay Airlines to launch first scheduled commercial flight to Bangkok in July, fleet expansion plans on hold amid Covid border measures
  • Honeymoon years (1997-2002): Hong Kong’s early period under Chinese rule in 25 photos
  • Hong Kong customs arrests 51-year-old woman on suspicion of laundering more than HK$350 million over 3 years
  • Hong Kong ranks 5th in list of world’s most competitive economies, climbing 2 spots over improved performance amid Covid pandemic
  • SCMP picks Catherine So as chief executive, tapping Hong Kong media and tech veteran to run one of Asia’s oldest English newspapers
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong reports over 1,000 cases for first time in more than 2 months as nightspot patrons cancel bookings ahead of new rule launch
  • Hong Kong legislators question transport bureau’s proposal for congestion levy to ease traffic jams at cross-harbour tunnels
  • Hong Kong slips one place to fourth most expensive city for luxury spending as hotel rates take a huge hit from pandemic, Julius Baer report finds
  • Hong Kong judges and barristers could wave goodbye to courtroom wigs in run-up to 2047, says city’s first chief justice
  • Exclusive interview: Hong Kong’s first chief justice Andrew Li on whether all judges in city should be Chinese nationals ahead of 2047
  • Climate Change: China’s firms lag behind Asia, North America, Europe in net-zero targets with only 4 per cent adherence, study finds
  • Historic Hong Kong mansion handed over to pro-Beijing think tank to transform into healthy living centre
  • February 8, 2016: Night of mob violence in Mong Kok leaves Hong Kong on edge
  • Beijing reveals blueprint for Nansha, with incentives that could draw young Hongkongers into calling the Guangzhou district home
  • Zuckerberg’s Meta to push metaverse initiatives to Hong Kong cafes and schools as interest in virtual world hots up
  • November 1, 2014: Expat banker Rurik Jutting arrested over murder of 2 women
  • HKMA sells US$3.24 billion to defend Hong Kong currency peg from capital outflow ahead of expected US interest rate rise
  • China’s bank runs highlight abuse among small-bank shareholders, despite crackdown
  • China coronavirus: ‘you expire in 3 days’ restrictions and negative test proof requirements hit daily life hard
  • Bar at centre of latest Beijing Covid cluster loses its licence as staff face criminal investigation
  • Ant Group’s payment services platform Alipay is most trusted brand in China, new survey by US firm finds
  • Why archaeologists are so excited by China’s Sanxingdui ruins
  • Taiwan-US security and military talks will discuss weapons and strategies to defend island against Beijing
  • China’s internet watchdog tightens mobile app rules for national security, requires promotion of ‘core socialist values’
  • As China’s economic slowdown moderates, analysts call for greater policy easing: ‘it will be a long haul back’
  • China refrains from policy rate cut, defying market expectations once again
  • How China’s belt and road diplomacy could end the Ukraine conflict for good
  • Veteran Chinese human rights lawyers expected to face trial soon
  • Chubb CEO Evan Greenberg urges US businesses to stay engaged in China market
  • Backlash against Russians living abroad is all too familiar for anxious Chinese expats
  • For China’s elite graduates, career dreams take a back seat to stability in coronavirus-hit job market
  • US drug supply chain alternative to China might run through Canada, Latin America
  • Chinese students’ dream device defeats Japan’s most powerful supercomputer in world contest
  • Dozens of countries call out China at UN over Xinjiang abuse allegations
  • Where did water on the moon come from? Chinese scientists find clues in lunar samples
  • Apple CEO Tim Cook expects continued growth for ‘cutting-edge’ Chinese software developers amid rising geopolitical tensions
  • China’s consumption problem rooted in fears and uncertainties, while vouchers have limited appeal
  • Chinese concerns rise over US policy shift on Taiwan
  • China still backing Poland gateway plan despite impact of Ukraine war
  • Career diplomat Le Yucheng moves to Chinese state broadcasting body as deputy chief
  • Coronavirus: Taiwan’s economic outlook dims as Omicron outbreak casts a shadow over growth
  • Indian forces in Kashmir kill militants suspected of targeted shooting, part of increased counter-insurgency effort
  • Japan, Australia to expand defence ties for regional order amid growing fear of China’s presence
  • Singapore ramps up ‘Wolbachia’ mosquito breeding as dengue crisis escalates
  • Polluted air cuts life expectancy by 2 years, South Asia and India most affected, China improving
  • OnlyFans couple sought in Thailand over ‘unacceptable’ sex videos that were leaked online
  • Indonesia’s Joko Widodo to meet Vladimir Putin in Moscow on June 30: Russian report
  • Asia’s energy crunch turns from bad to worse after fire at US plant
  • Australia-China relations: Canberra’s foreign minister Penny Wong to travel to Solomon Islands amid China security pact concerns
  • Deaf Indian boy trapped in well with snakes and scorpions is rescued after four days
  • Coronavirus: New Zealand to eliminate pre-departure Covid-19 tests for incoming passengers
  • India all but sinks WTO sustainable fishing deal; China among contributors to global overfishing
  • Can Sri Lanka tourism recover from ‘triple whammy’ of terrorism, Covid-19 and economic crisis?
  • BTS announce temporary break, with plans for solo releases before reuniting as group – though label denies it’s a ‘hiatus’
  • Are India’s billion-dollar trade ties with Gulf states in ‘peril’ amid Prophet Mohammed row?
  • Thai cat with Covid suspected to have infected human
  • WHO will share vaccines to stop monkeypox amid inequity fears
  • Sweltering heat stifles a third of US population, severe storms leave thousands in the dark
  • Joe Biden gambles there’s more to gain by visiting ‘pariah’ Saudi Arabia
  • Ukraine fights on after Russia’s surrender ultimatum, evacuation offer for Sievierodonetsk holdouts
  • Who’s coming to Putin’s ‘Russian Davos’ in St Petersburg?
  • Russia turns to old tanks as it burns through weapons in Ukraine, European officials say
  • In Ukraine-Russia war, echoes of WWI with artillery duels, trenches and shell-blasted landscapes
  • Netflix plans Squid Game reality show with US$4.5 million prize, no fatal consequences
  • Canada and Denmark settle decades-old ‘whiskey war’ over Arctic island
  • UK vows to pursue asylum seeker policy after first deportation flight to Rwanda cancelled
  • Iran preparing for rocket launch, satellite images suggest amid nuclear tensions
  • Jailed Putin critic Alexei Navalny moved to high-security penal colony
  • Happy the elephant is not a person, top New York court rules – she is a ‘magnificent creature’
  • EU turns to Israel for gas after ‘blackmail’ by Russia over Ukraine war support
  • Pope says Russia’s ‘ferocious’ invasion perhaps provoked and we don’t see the ‘whole drama’
  • China’s semiconductor output in May rebounds as Covid-19 restrictions ease
  • Why rising interest rates are bad news for Hong Kong’s housing market
  • Google debate over sentient AI overshadows more pressing issues like prejudice and exploitation, researchers say
  • Emigrating mainland Chinese and Hongkongers could take combined wealth of US$65 billion with them, consultancy says
  • China’s US$20 trillion asset management industry to embrace more diverse products amid revamp, think tank says
  • Alibaba leads Hong Kong stocks to best gain in a week on China data while markets brace for Fed rate hike
  • US likely to single out Tesla for crashes involving Autopilot after collecting data on automated driving
  • China releases new national climate change strategy to build resilience against global warming by 2035
  • China to test market appetite for debt with biggest offshore bond sale since 2016
  • In Hong Kong’s property market, HK$10 million no longer buys what it did in 1997
  • Developers’ aggressive loan schemes could put financially weak buyers at risk if Hong Kong home prices fall, analysts say
  • Hong Kong malls, retailers tap into AI analytics to get smarter after losing US$16 billion of sales in post-2018 industry slump
  • China’s 618 online shopping festival more muted than previous years as platforms shift focus from sales to service quality
  • As the industrial metaverse grows, the platform must remain open and available
  • Yuan currency most exposed to climate change – could lose half its value in 50 years, Barclays says
  • US senator questions whether HSBC was pressured to suspend banker over controversial climate-change comments
  • SoftBank plans additional London listing for Arm initial public offering
  • ByteDance’s Hong Kong entity registers multiple mainland subsidiaries in move seen as prelude to possible listing in the city
  • Netflix’s 2022 Iron Chef reboot pits culinary legends against rising talents in quest to be the best in the TV kitchen
  • Hong Kong Disneyland launches new fireworks show featuring lasers, water fountains and projections beamed onto Castle of Magical Dreams
  • Best K-pop albums and singles of 2022 so far, from That That by Psy and BTS’ Suga to Stray Kids’ Oddinary and Dreamcatcher’s Apocalypse
  • Just Remembering movie review: Japanese youth romance starring Sairi Ito and Sosuke Ikematsu sees a relationship develop in reverse
  • From minimalism to maximalist design, Singapore apartment full of vintage furniture, collectibles and art is a delight for the senses
  • The best cart noodles in Hong Kong according to a chef, where he goes for spare ribs rice, and memorable meals with his boss, ‘Demon Chef’ Alvin Leung
  • Airport chaos as surge in flight bookings catches travel trade on the hop, with long queues and cancellations
  • What is amla? The Indian gooseberry hailed as a superfood for its apparent anticancer, anti-diabetes and anti-ageing properties
  • The Black Phone movie review: Ethan Hawke plays a sadistic child murderer in Doctor Strange director’s effective horror thriller
  • Are bleached eyebrows right for you? Expert tips on a viral ’90s beauty trend made popular by Kendall Jenner, Maisie Williams and Lizzo
  • Netflix K-drama review: Our Blues – Lee Byung-hun and Kim Hye-ja provide heart-rending ending for ensemble drama
  • Why luxury hotels are all about mental health in 2022: forget five-star spas and Michelin-starred restaurants, guests now demand therapy sessions and on-site counsellors from a wellness retreat
  • 6 K-pop groups who stayed together after leaving their agencies: Got7, 2PM, Girls’ Generation, Mamamoo, Apink and Highlight all made comebacks with their OG members
  • Meet Kim Se-jeong, Today’s Webtoon star: the K-drama actress debuted as a K-pop idol in IOI before acting in K-dramas like Business Proposal on Netflix – but why is she called ‘Korea’s Emma Stone’?
  • Zhou Rui’s provocative fashion: she’s dressed Blackpink’s Lisa, Madison Beer and other stars within just three years of launching her brand thanks to her style that ‘isn’t limited by gender or size’
  • Meet Riley Keough, Elvis Presley’s granddaughter – she modelled for Dior before acting and directing, and ‘loved’ stepdad Michael Jackson, but why didn’t she act in the Elvis biopic?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • More Japanese people choose to remain single, report shows
  • Tokyo logs 1st week-on-week rise in new cases in over a month
  • New law gives adult film stars the right to end their contracts
  • Dozens of fake city websites dominate Bing search results
  • Travel discount program to continue, expand from July 1
  • Parco store apologizes for using ‘sex booth’ to promote event
  • Tokyo dentist bakes bread you can really sink your teeth into
  • Tokyo posts 1,528 new COVID-19 cases and 6 related deaths
  • Train crew gets training in knife rampage drill on Shinkansen
  • Evacuees from nuclear disaster await Supreme Court ruling

  • Japan philanthropic group begins fundraising for Ukrainians
  • Kishida Cabinet approves July 10 as date for Upper House election

  • Diet fails to draw up rules on use of lawmakers’ ‘second salaries’
  • Diet members’ allowances still being spent with no checks
  • Idemitsu to close one of its biggest petroleum refineries in 2024
  • iPhone prices in Japan found lowest in global survey

  • Pix of Japanese wearing masks in demand for AI training

  • China city mayor apologizes over COVID-19 lockdown response
  • North Korea asks China to resume its supply freight train services
  • South Korean truckers end 8-day strike, return to work
  • North Korea spent as much as $642 million on nukes in 2021, report estimates
  • U.S. to keep up North Korea pressure; Seoul urges China to use influence
  • South Korea truckers’ strike has local shocks, not global yet
  • South Korea says North completed prep for new nuclear test
  • Bangkok cannabis pop-up truck proves popular with tourists
  • China attack on Taiwan would hit global trade more than Ukraine war, says Taiwan
  • U.K. cancels first flight to deport asylum seekers to Rwanda
  • Australia wants ‘broad, deep’ security ties with Japan, minister says
  • So long, Internet Explorer. The browser is finally retiring
  • Markets tumble worldwide, bear market growls on Wall Street
  • U.S. Defense Secretary Austin meets Thai leader to boost ties
  • Bitcoin plunges as major crypto lender halts operations
  • Precision COVID-19 test devised to finish diagnoses in just 9 minutes
  • K-pop supergroup BTS says it’s making time for solo projects
  • Sapporo classical music fest to be held in full for the 1st time in 3 years
  • Go champ Iyama claims record 11th straight Honinbo title
  • Ninja gym for learning fabled skills opens year-round in Iga
  • EDITORIAL: Kishida must return to his vow to tackle societal wealth disparity
  • EDITORIAL: Revised penal code should help in rehabilitation of prison inmates
  • VOX POPULI: New agency needed to cure inflation rather than diseases
  • VOX POPULI: Remembering the catastrophic Sanriku tsunami of June 15, 1896
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Biden Weighs Tariff Rollback to Ease Inflation, Even a Little Bit
